primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility for hedge accounting of foreign exchange exposure arising from intragroup dividend receivable in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs hedge accounting eligibility by restricting hedged items to those with external parties and prohibiting hedge accounting for intragroup transactions at consolidated level except for investment entities
Provides guidance on hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations, which is the relevant framework for foreign currency risk hedges at consolidated level

supporting_authority.reason: Defines separate financial statements where investment entity exception applies and provides context for distinguishing consolidated vs separate level accounting
Defines investment entity concept that creates the exception to elimination of intragroup transactions for hedge accounting purposes

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosure and elimination of intragroup transactions but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for foreign exchange exposures
Deals with disclosure of interests in subsidiaries and structured entities, not directly relevant to hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup exposures

treatment_families.family: General prohibition on hedge accounting for intragroup exposures at consolidated level
Investment entity exception permitting hedge accounting for intragroup exposures at consolidated level
Net investment hedge framework for foreign currency exposures in consolidated statements

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup foreign exchange exposures at consolidated level
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly restricts hedge accounting to items with external parties, making intragroup dividend receivables ineligible for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements except for investment entities

approaches.label: Investment entity exception for intragroup hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 provides an exception for investment entities where intragroup transactions measured at fair value through profit or loss are not eliminated, permitting hedge accounting for such exposures at consolidated level

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting for foreign currency exposures
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 provides the framework for hedges of net investments in foreign operations, which is the appropriate mechanism for foreign currency risk arising from net assets in consolidated statements, though it applies to net assets rather than individual dividend receivables
